data.title: US Consumer Prices Rise 0.3% in September
data.description:
US consumer prices rose 0.3% month-over-month in September 2025, easing slightly from August’s 0.4% gain and just below expectations of 0.4%.

Energy costs climbed 1.5%, led by a 4.1% surge in gasoline, while food prices increased 0.2%.

Additional upward pressure came from shelter, airline fares, recreation, household goods, and apparel.

These gains were partly offset by lower motor vehicle insurance, used car, and communication prices.

Core CPI, which excludes food and energy, rose 0.2%, just below August's pace and slightly below forecasts of 0.3%.

data.title: KazMunayGas closes order book for its debut issue of yuan-denominated Eurobonds.
data.description:
Kazakhstan's state-owned oil and gas company KazMunayGas (KMG) announced on Friday that it had closed the order book for its debut issue of Chinese yuan-denominated Eurobonds in the amount of 1.25 billion yuan ($175.49 million).

The bonds will have a five-year maturity, a coupon rate of 2.950%, and a yield of 3.150%.

The debut issue is part of KMG's recently established 10 billion yuan medium-term notes program. The placement is scheduled for October 30, 2025.

($1 = 7.1230 Chinese yuan renminbi)

data.title: Dolphin Drilling – Legacy UK Tax Claim
data.description:
OSLO, Norway, 24th October 2025 - Dolphin Drilling AS (OSE: DDRIL) ("Dolphin") today confirms that His Majesty’s Revenue and Customs (“HMRC”) in the UK and the Company has reached a position in respect of the outstanding corporation tax liability of GBP 12.2 million, where the Company will repay its debt to HMRC over a period until April 30, 2026. This liability relates to a legacy claim dismissed by the UK Supreme Court on 24 June 2025. Dolphin Drilling is committed to meeting its financial obligations and continuing to focus on its operational and strategic priorities.

Contacts:

Ingolf Gillesdal, CFO

Phone: +47 920 45 320

Email: Ingolf.gillesdal@dolphindrilling.com

About Dolphin Drilling AS:

Dolphin Drilling AS is an Oslo listed, Aberdeen head-quartered, company which owns and operates a fleet of harsh environment mid-water & deep-water semisubmersible drilling rigs, capable of working worldwide.

data.title: Beyond Meat expects to post quarterly revenue above estimates
data.description:
Beyond Meat said on Friday it expects to record third-quarter revenue above analysts' expectations, at a time when the faux meat maker's highly shorted shares have been on a rise.

The company said it expects quarterly revenue of $70 million, above estimates of $68.9 million, according to data compiled by LSEG.

Shares of the company have risen nearly 500% in the last week, fueling a fresh wave of buying among retail traders who have sparked meme stock frenzies on Wall Street in recent years.

The company also expects quarterly operating expenses to be in the range of $41 million to $43 million for the three months ended September 27, while also flagging a significant impairment charge.

data.title: US Inflation Comes in Lower Than Expected
data.description:
The annual inflation rate in the US rose to 3% in September 2025, the highest since January, from 2.9% in August and below forecasts of 3.1%.

The energy index increased 2.8% and the food index increased 3.1%.

Meanwhile, core inflation actually slowed to 3% from 3.1%, with markets expecting it to stay at 3.1%.

Compared to the previous month, the CPI increased 0.3%, below 0.4% in August and forecasts of 0.4%.

The index for gasoline rose 4.1% and was the largest factor in the all items monthly increase.

data.title: Inspire Medical Systems dips as Jefferies cuts to 'hold'
data.description:
** Shares of medtech company Inspire Medical Systems fall nearly 3% to $77.9 in premarket

** Jefferies downgrades stock rating to "hold" from "buy"

* Cites reduced confidence in co’s ability to accelerate sales growth to medium-term targets, and no longer expects valuation upside from multiple expansion.

** Brokerage also slashes PT to $85 from $160, a 6% upside to the stock's last close

** Jefferies says ongoing headwinds from GLP-1 weight-loss drugs, rising competition and market share losses, weak physician reimbursement, and limited expectations for Inspire 5 (co’s next-generation sleep apnea device) are likely to hinder sales growth acceleration in 2026

** Average rating of 20 analysts is "buy"; median PT $125 - data compiled by LSEG

** As of last close, co down nearly 57% YTD

data.title: Russia's Central Bank Cuts Key Rate as New Sanctions Loom — 2nd Update
data.description:
By Paul Hannon

Russia's central bank on Friday lowered its key interest rate for a fourth straight meeting as an already slowing economy braces for the impact of fresh sanctions from the U.S. and the European Union in response to President Vladimir Putin's continued war on Ukraine.

The Bank of Russia cut its key rate to 16.5% from 17%, having begun to lower borrowing costs from a recent peak of 21% in June. The move was smaller than previous cuts.

The central bank is loosening the restraints it placed on activity to prevent the economy from overheating as the government spent heavily on its full-scale invasion of Ukraine and workers became scarce. In essence, the central bank has cooled demand in the rest of the economy to offset the increase related to the war.

Despite the sequential cuts, it continued to describe its policy as "tight," a term that indicates that borrowing costs are still high enough to damp activity.

"Last year's economic overheating is gradually subsiding," Bank of Russia Gov. Elvira Nabiullina told reporters after the decision was announced. "This process remains unfinished."

That is one factor driving a slowdown in the Russian economy this year. Even as it raised its forecasts for global economic growth earlier this month, the International Monetary Fund again lowered its projections for Russia.

It now sees the country's economy growing by just 0.6% this year, having expected to see a 1.5% expansion in April. The Bank of Russia Friday lowered its growth forecast for this year to a range between 0.5% and 1%, having previously seen an expansion of between 1% and 2%.

The IMF's forecasts were published before the U.S. government Wednesday announced a fresh round of sanctions designed to lower the Russian government's revenues from oil exports, a major source of funding for its prosecution of the war.

The new sanctions, which would be the first direct U.S. measures on Russia during the second Trump administration, target Lukoil and Rosneft.

The U.S. sanctions could bar foreign countries or companies from conducting business with the oil companies and cut them off from much of the international financial system.

Separately, the EU Thursday announced its nineteenth round of sanctions against Russia, including a ban on imports of liquefied natural gas and measures aimed at two Chinese refineries that buy Russian crude oil.

Nabiullina said the sanctions will have an impact on the economy, although it is difficult to judge exactly how large that will be.

"We are looking at it as a negative external factor," Nabiullina said.

The Russian government's oil revenues have already been hit by low prices, with the result that its budget deficit has been wider than planned.

That has prompted the government to announce a rise in some taxes next year, including an increase in sales tax that is likely to push inflation higher. However, the central bank expects inflation to resume its fall after the immediate impact of the tax increase.

"The expected increase in taxes will help bring inflation down over the medium term," Nabiullina said.

Write to Paul Hannon at paul.hannon@wsj.com

data.title: US Core CPI Rises Less than Expected
data.description:
Core consumer prices in the US, which exclude food and energy, rose by 0.2% from the previous month in September of 2025, slowing from the 0.3% in the August and July, and slightly under market expectations of a 0.3% increase.

The data was released with weeks of delay as the ongoing US government shutdown suspended activity in the Bureau of Labor Statistics.

Prices rose slower for shelter (0.2% vs 0.4% in August), transportation services (0.3% vs 1%), and new vehicles (0.2% vs 0.3%).

In turn, the CPI rebounded for medical care services (0.3% vs -0.1%) and accelerate for apparel (0.7% vs 0.5%).

From the pervious year, core consumer prices rose by 3% in September.

data.description:

data.title: Myers Industries Announces Quarterly Dividend
data.description:
AKRON, Ohio--(BUSINESS WIRE)--October 24, 2025--

Myers Industries, Inc. today announced that its Board of Directors has approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.135 per share, payable on January 5, 2026, to shareholders of record as of December 3, 2025.

About Myers Industries

Myers Industries Inc., based in Akron, Ohio, is a leading manufacturer of sustainable plastic and metal products that protect the world from the ground up for Consumer, Vehicle, Food & Beverage, Industrial, Infrastructure, and Automotive Aftermarket end markets. Myers Industries has a rich history that is built on strong brands and innovative products. Through years of continuous product development and strategic acquisitions, we have established ourselves as a leading diversified industrial company. We provide critical solutions to our customers, delivering exceptional value. Visit www.myersindustries.com to learn more.

M-INV

Senior Director of Investor Relations

(252) 536-5651

data.title: Labor Smart, Inc. (doing business as Kultura Brands, Inc.) Retires 500 million Common Shares; Confirms 5 billion Share Retirement Initiative Underway
data.description:
Kultura Brands, Inc., today announced that 500,000,000 (five hundred million) common shares of its stock have been officially retired and removed from the company's issued and outstanding shares.

JACKSON, WY / ACCESS Newswire / October 24, 2025 / Labor Smart, Inc. (OTCID:LTNC), doing business as Kultura Brands, Inc., today announced that 500,000,000 (five hundred million) common shares of its stock have been officially retired and removed from the company's issued and outstanding shares, as confirmed by Empire Stock Transfer on October 23, 2025.

This completed retirement marks the first phase of the company's broader 5 billion share reduction plan, which is expected to be executed over the next 30 to 60 days.

A Shift Toward Verified, Post-Completion Reporting

In response to shareholder feedback and concerns regarding prior share retirement announcements made before completion, the company has adopted a new transparency policy. Under this policy, Kultura Brands, will only announce share retirements after the process has been fully executed and verified by the company's transfer agent with the transaction journal. "Our focus is on transparency backed by verification," said Brad Wyatt, Chief Executive Officer. "We understand the frustration surrounding premature announcements in the past. Moving forward, our communications will reflect only completed, documented actions when it comes to share retirement. The 500 million share retirement is finished, verified, and represents the first 10% of our 5 billion share reduction goal." This initiative will include both common and preferred shares.

Commitment to Completion and Clarity

The remaining 4.5 billion shares targeted for retirement are tied to specific agreements and compliance steps that must be finalized before cancellation. These include the completion of various shareholder settlements, asset transfer agreements, conversions, and other obligations associated with the return of shares. The 4.5B shares remaining comprise of both Preferred and Common shares to be retired. With full transparency, we are still working through cashflow management with the large projects at hand that are yet to demonstrate full profitability potential and requires continued private placements and cleanup of past investments for equity (share issuance), all being part of the larger share reform plan. We will see this stabilize and the capitalization table consolidate through the efforts of the ongoing share retirements and the operational revenue kicking in with increased sales volume and profitability.

Upon completion of each phase, the company will release via "X" (@KulturaBrands) and other social media venues the corresponding transaction journal directly from Empire Stock Transfer as confirmation of share retirement.

"We're focused on accountability through documentation," added Wyatt. "We are committed to responsibly reducing our outstanding share count and reporting results only after the retirement is complete and confirmed."

About Kultura Brands, Inc.

Kultura Brands Inc. is a Wyoming-based public company (OTCID:LTNC) focused on identifying, building, and scaling culture-driven brands. With a foundation rooted in operational discipline and services, our refreshed strategy moves us into brand acquisition, development, and community-led growth, aimed at creating long-term value for consumers and shareholders alike.

Forward-Looking Statements

This press release includes statements regarding the Company's intentions and strategic direction. Although the Company has taken steps as described, many of the matters referenced (including the timing of share retirement and the dependence on individuals to complete their part of the process) are subject to risks and uncertainties. There can be no assurance that requests will be approved in the timeline or manner described. The Company assumes no obligation to update or revise any statements in this release, except as required by law. This press release contains forward-looking statements. In addition, from time to time, we or our representatives may make forward-looking statements orally or in writing. We base these forward-looking statements on our expectations and projections about future events, which we derive from the information currently available to us. Such forward-looking statements relate to future events or our future performance, including our financial performance and projections, revenue and earnings growth, and business prospects and opportunities. You can identify forward-looking statements by those that are not historical in nature, particularly those that use terminology such as "may," "should," "expects," "anticipates," "contemplates," "estimates," "believes," "plans," "projected," "predicts," "potential," or "hopes" or the negative of these or similar terms. In evaluating these forward-looking statements, you should consider various factors, including: (i) potential failure to meet projected development and related targets; (ii) changes in applicable laws or regulations that may impact our products and business; and (iii) other risks and uncertainties described herein, as well as those risks and uncertainties discussed from time to time in other reports and other public filings with OTC Markets. These and other factors may cause our actual results to differ materially from any forward-looking statement. We are not obligated to publicly update or revise any forward-looking statement, whether as a result of uncertainties and assumptions, the forward-looking events discussed in this press release and other statements made from time to time by us or our representatives might not occur.

data.title: KALENDER-Termine Politik/Vermischtes bis 2. November
data.description:
*************************************************************

Weitere ausführliche Terminübersichten erhalten sie unter:

* FIRMEN/MÄRKTE

* KONJUNKTUR/GELDPOLITIK

*************************************************************

Alle Zeitangaben in Central European Time (CET)

SATURDAY OCTOBER 25, 2025

Ivory Coast holds presidential election

Counting begins in Irish presidential election - Counting begins in the election of Ireland's next president with Catherine Connolly, an independent leftwing lawmaker backed by Ireland's main opposition parties and one of the leading pro-Palestinian voices in parliament, the clear favourite to defeat a government-backed candidate. A winner for the largely ceremonial role may be declared on Saturday or run into Sunday.

Malaysia hosts an ASEAN business forum - Malaysia will host a business and investment forum on the sidelines of a meeting of leaders from the Association of Southeast Asian Nations and trading partners. Leaders from Malaysia, Brazil, South Africa, Canada and the Philippines are expected to speak at the business forum.

Vietnam hosts signing ceremony of UN Convention against cybercrime - Vietnam hosts the signing ceremony of the U.N. Convention against cybercrime, with the U.N. Secretary General Antonio Guterres expected to deliver a speech at the opening session

04:30 - Malaysian, Brazilian leaders hold bilateral talks on the sidelines of the ASEAN Summit - Malaysian Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim and Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva hold bilateral talks on the sidelines of the 47th ASEAN Leaders' Summit in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia.

12:00 - German utility RWE detonates cooling towers of disused nuclear plant - The two cooling towers of the decommissioned German Gundremmingen nuclear plant with a height of 160 m (525 ft.) each are to be detonated to make room for the storage of dismantled components of the plant.

SUNDAY OCTOBER 26, 2025

ASEAN, world leaders arrive at the venue of the 47th ASEAN Leaders' Summit

Leaders of the Southeast Asian regional bloc and partner countries arrive at the venue of the 47th ASEAN Leaders' Summit in Kuala Lumpur.

Live view of Kuala Lumpur landmarks as US President Trump arrives for ASEAN Summit

Leaders of ASEAN and India meet during a high level summit in Kuala Lumpur

Leaders of the ASEAN regional bloc meet with Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi during the 47th ASEAN Leaders' Summit in Kuala Lumpur.

Bangkok and Phnom Penh locals react to ceasefire agreement

Locals in Bangkok and Phnom Penh assess the ceasefire agreement between Thailand and Cambodia after it was signed on the sidelines of the ASEAN regional bloc's meeting.

First day of the 47th ASEAN summit in Kuala Lumpur

Leaders from ASEAN and its dialogue partners convene in Kuala Lumpur for a series of high-level summits.

Trump, Brazil's Lula to meet in Malaysia

Brazil's President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva and U.S. President Donald Trump will meet in Malaysia on Sunday, O Globo newspaper reported late on Tuesday, citing diplomatic sources. The White House has not announced a meeting between the leaders.

ASEAN leaders admit East Timor into regional block

Leaders of ASEAN nations sign the declaration of admission for East Timor during the 47th ASEAN Leaders' Summit in Kuala Lumpur.

US President Trump arrives in Kuala Lumpur for the 47th ASEAN Leaders' Summit

US President Donald Trump arrives in Kuala Lumpur for the 47th ASEAN Leaders' Summit, where he will join in a series of high level meetings.

Patrick Herminie sworn in as Seychelles’ sixth president after run-off win

Patrick Herminie will be sworn in as Seychelles’ sixth president in Victoria after winning a run-off election with 52.7% of the vote. His United Seychelles party also secured a parliamentary majority, marking a political comeback for both Herminie and the party.

Deadline passes for Kosovo’s PM Kurti to form new government

The deadline passes for Kosovo’s Prime Minister Albin Kurti to form a new government, following inconclusive elections in February that plunged the country into its deepest political crisis since independence in 2008.

Malaysia hosts an ASEAN business forum

Malaysia will host a business and investment forum on the sidelines of a meeting of leaders from the Association of Southeast Asian Nations and trading partners. Leaders from Malaysia, Brazil, South Africa, Canada and the Philippines are expected to speak at the business forum.

Leaders of ASEAN and Japan meet during a high level summit in Kuala Lumpur

Leaders of the ASEAN regional bloc meet with Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi during the 47th ASEAN Leaders' Summit in Kuala Lumpur.

ASEAN, world leaders kick off the 47th ASEAN Leaders' Summit in Kuala Lumpur

Leaders of ASEAN nations and partner countries attend the opening ceremony of the 47th ASEAN Leaders' Summit in Kuala Lumpur.

Opening remarks of the 47th ASEAN Leaders' Summit plenary session

Leaders of ASEAN nations and partner countries speak at the plenary session of the 47th ASEAN Leaders' Summit in Kuala Lumpur.

Trump may attend ASEAN summit in Malaysia

U.S. President Donald Trump will visit Malaysia on Sunday and may facilitate a ceasefire agreement between Thailand and Cambodia on the sidelines of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations summit, according to Malaysian Foreign Minister Mohamad Hasan. Trump will meet the prime minister of Malaysia in the afternoon and attend the ASEAN Leaders Working Dinner that evening, the White House said.

07:00 - Malaysia PM holds bilateral with Trump

Malaysian Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim holds a bilateral meeting with U.S. President Donald Trump on the sidelines of the ASEAN regional bloc's summit. Both leaders are expected to witness the signing of a trade deal between the countries.

09:00 - Thailand, Cambodia to sign ceasefire deal at ASEAN summit

Thailand and Cambodia are expected to sign a broader ceasefire agreement for their border dispute on the sidelines of the ASEAN regional bloc's meeting. U.S. President Donald Trump and Malaysian premier Anwar Ibrahim will reportedly witness the signing.

10:00 - Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu convenes cabinet meeting

Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu convenes cabinet meeting.

10:00 - Malaysians stage protest against Trump’s attendance at ASEAN summit

Malaysians in Kuala Lumpur protest the attendance of U.S. President Donald Trump at the ASEAN summit.

11:00 - Trump attends ASEAN-U.S. summit

U.S. President Donald Trump is expected to attend a meeting with regional counterparts from the Association of Southeast Asian Nations.

11:30 - Argentina holds national midterm election

11:45 - Polls open in Argentina for national midterm election

13:00 - Argentina’s opposition leader Kicillof casts his vote at national midterm election

16:00 - Argentina’s President Milei casts his vote at national midterm election

23:00 - Sanders, AOC support mayoral candidate Mamdani at New York rally

Progressive Senator Bernie Sanders and congresswoman Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez attend a "New York is Not for Sale" rally in Queens to support New York mayoral candidate Zohran Mamdani ahead of the November 4 election.

23:00 - Argentina’s President Milei addresses his supporters after national midterm election

23:30 - Argentina’s opposition leader Kicillof addresses his supporters after national midterm election

MONDAY OCTOBER 27, 2025

A look at New York mayoral candidate Andrew Cuomo

A look at Independent New York City mayoral candidate Andrew Cuomo ahead of the November 4 election. Cuomo is a former three-term New York governor who was defeated by Zohran Mamdani in the Democratic primary but is still in the race, running as an independent.

Cameroon election results

Cameroon's constitutional council is set to proclaim the result of the Oct. 12 presidential election.

CITYSHOTS: Gyeongju, host of this year's APEC summit

Once known mainly for school trips, Gyeongju is gearing up to host APEC—showcasing its UNESCO sites and cultural heritage to the world.

Trump leaves Malaysia after the the 47th ASEAN Leaders' Summit

U.S. President Donald Trump leaves Malaysia after the 47th ASEAN Leaders' Summit, where he joined in a series of high level meetings.

ASEAN, world leaders attend a gala dinner during the 47th ASEAN Summit in Kuala Lumpur

Leaders of the ASEAN regional bloc and partner countries attend a gala dinner during the 47th ASEAN Leaders Summit in Kuala Lumpur.

Little-known South Korean city Gyeongju takes global stage as APEC host

Long seen as little more than a destination for school trips, Gyeongju is now preparing to host APEC and welcome world leaders. Local officials and residents are ramping up efforts to showcase the city’s UNESCO World Heritage sites and rich cultural legacy to a global audience.

A look at New York mayoral candidate Curtis Sliwa

A look at Republican mayoral candidate Curtis Sliwa ahead of the November 4 election. Curtis Sliwa is a longtime New York City activist and founder of the Guardian Angels, known for his public safety advocacy and previous Republican mayoral run in 2021.

Reaction and analysis of results from the national midterm election

Security ramped up in Tokyo ahead of Trump arrival

Japan deploys about 18,000 security personnel to patrol on streets in Tokyo ahead of Trump's visit.

EXPLAINER: What is APEC? What is it all about?

EXPLAINER: What is APEC? What is its history and evolving role?

A look at New York mayoral candidate Zohran Mamdani

A look at Democratic New York mayoral candidate Zohran Mamdani ahead of the November 4 election. Zohran Mamdani is a 33-year-old New York State Assemblymember and self-described democratic socialist who won the Democratic mayoral primary in a surprise upset over Andrew Cuomo, campaigning on affordability, rent freezes, and public ownership initiatives.

What's on the agenda of this year's APEC summit in Gyeongju?

Global attention is turning to Gyeongju as it prepares to host the 2025 APEC Summit—an event that could become a pivotal stage for geopolitics. With the possibility of a U.S.–China leaders' bilateral and even faint prospects of U.S.–North Korea dialogue, the summit may offer clues to the next chapter in global trade and security dynamics.

TIMELINE: Key moments in U.S.-China trade relations during Trump's presidency

A timeline details key moments in U.S.-China trade relations during U.S. President Donald Trump's presidency after he said on Wednesday he expected to reach agreements with Chinese President Xi Jinping when they meet in South Korea this week.

U.S. President Donald Trump arrives in Japan

U.S. President Donald Trump is expected to arrive in Japan to meet the country's new Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi.

REFILE : Will a Dutch town that is historically far-right, turn away from Wilders?

Filming in a picturesque Dutch town that voted for far-right leader Geert Wilders in the last election, but polls show some may be turning away from his party, which is currently still leading in polls.

Deadline for Trump to file amended appeal in hush money criminal case

Donald Trump's personal lawyers face an Oct. 27 deadline to file an amended appeal of the U.S. president's criminal conviction last year on New York state charges of falsifying business records to cover up hush money paid to a porn star. Coverage on merit.

Second day of the 47th ASEAN summit in Kuala Lumpur

Leaders from ASEAN and its dialogue partners convene for a second day in Malaysian capital Kuala Lumpur for a series of high-level summits.

Argentine financial markets ract to midterm election results

Argentina's financial markets react to results of a midterm election in which President Javier Milei is aiming to increase his party's seats in both chambers of the legislature.

09:45 - UN secretary-general holds press conference at ASEAN leaders' summit

United Nations Secretary-General Antonio Guterres holds a press conference ahead of his attendance to a meeting with leaders from the Association of Southeast Asian Nations.

11:00 - President Steinmeier receives Luxembourg's Grand Duke Guillaume

German President Frank Walter Steinmeier receives Luxembourg's Grand Duke Guillaume in Berlin with military honors

TUESDAY OCTOBER 28, 2025

Third and final day of the 47th ASEAN summit in Kuala Lumpur

Leaders from ASEAN and its dialogue partners convene for a third day in Malaysian capital Kuala Lumpur for a series of high-level summits.

UN publishes annual assessment of countries' climate pledges

The UN's climate body (UNFCCC) publishes its annual assessment of countries' climate change commitments and how much global warming the world will face if these pledges are met.

Malaysian PM Anwar gives news conference after ASEAN Leaders' Summit

Malaysian Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim delivers a news conference at the end of the 47th ASEAN Leaders' Summit in Kuala Lumpur.

Dutch election candidates campaign ahead of polls

Dutch election candidates including Geert Wilders campaign ahead of the October 29 election after a government collapse triggered by the far right.

Malaysia hands over ASEAN chairmanship to Philippines on last day of leaders' summit

Malaysia hands over chairmanship of the ASEAN regional bloc to Philippines on the last day of the 47th ASEAN Leaders' Summit in Kuala Lumpur.

ASEAN, world leaders depart from Kuala Lumpur following the 47th ASEAN Leaders' Summit

Leaders of the Southeast Asian regional bloc and partner countries depart Kuala Lumpur International Airport after the 47th ASEAN Leaders' Summit.

Team USA hosts media summit ahead of 2026 Winter Games

One hundred days out from the 2026 Winter Games Milano Cortina, Team USA hosts a media summit in New York.

U.S. President Donald Trump holds talks with Japan's new PM

U.S. President Donald Trump is expected to hold bilateral talks with new Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi during a visit to Japan from October 27 to 29, according to chief Japanese cabinet secretary Minoru Kihara. Talks with Takaichi will take place on Tuesday morning, the White House said.

00:00 - South Korea-GDP Advance

09:30 - Germany’s economy minister takes part in Foreign Trade Day

German Economy Minister Katherina Reiche gives a speech and takes part in the opening panel at the Foreign Trade Day event focused on opportunities for German foreign trade amid geopolitical tensions.

20:00 - Reuters/Ipsos polling on Americans' view of President Donald Trump

A Reuters/Ipsos poll looks at American approval of President Donald Trump's performance.

20:30 - Dutch election candidates clash in tv debate

Dutch election candidates including far right leader Geert Wilders and former EU climate chief Frans Timmermans attend a televised debate ahead of Netherlands' October 29 election Just before Dutch citizens go to the polls, NOS traditionally holds a final debate with the leading candidates of political parties. First, the leading candidates of the six smaller parties debate, moderated by Winfried Baijens: ChristenUnie, FVD, DENK, SGP, Party for the Animals, and Volt. Then follows the NOS Journaal. Following that, the leading candidates of the nine larger parties debate, moderated by Rob Trip: PVV, GroenLinks-PvdA, VVD, CDA, BBB, NSC, D66, JA21, and SP.

WEDNESDAY OCTOBER 29, 2025

Team USA hosts media summit ahead of 2026 Winter Games

One hundred days out from the 2026 Winter Games Milano Cortina, Team USA hosts a media summit in New York.

Trump meets South Korea's Lee and attends APEC events

U.S. President Donald Trump will meet South Korean President Lee Jae Myung ahead of the APEC summit in Gyeongju, the White House said. He'll also deliver keynote remarks at the APEC CEO luncheon and participate in a U.S.-APEC leaders' working dinner.

Opening ceremony of the Paris Peace Forum

The Paris Peace Forum in Paris

Tanzania holds general elections

Tanzania is scheduled to hold general elections with President Samia Suluhu Hassan seeking re-election. The electoral process has been marked by significant controversy, including the barring of the main opposition Chadema party and concerns from human rights organizations about the fairness of the election

File footage of Trump and Xi meeting ahead of South Korean summit

File footage of previous meetings between US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping's ahead of their expected bilateral on the sidelines of the APEC Summit in South Kore

Leaders arrive South Korea ahead of APEC meeting

Leaders of APEC countries arrive South Korea ahead of the annual meeting

South Korea beef up security ahead of APEC Leaders' Summit

South Korea tighten security in the city of Gyeongju ahead of APEC Leaders' Summit

U.S. President Donald Trump leaves Japan

U.S. President Donald Trump leaves Japan to attend APEC summit in South Korea.

Anti-migration protest at asylum center near Amsterdam

Protesters are expected to gather around an asylum seeker center in Zaandam, near Amsterdam, on election day.

APEC CEO summit opens in Gyeongju

APEC CEO summit kicks off with business leaders to discuss various issues including energy transition, digital and AI transformation, trade and biotechnology.

The CDA political party will hold the results evening for the 2025 House of Representatives elections

The CDA political party will hold the results evening for the 2025 House of Representatives elections in Scheveningen. List leader Henri Bontenbal will give a speech. Program 4:30 PM: Setup for live television 7:30 PM: Reception of guests and press 9:00 PM: First exit poll 10:00 PM – 10:30 PM: Speech by Henri Bontenbal (times subject to change) 10:30 PM – 1:00 AM: Drinks and interviews Location: Beach Pavilion EVE, Strandweg 180, 2586 JM, Scheveningen

Dutch hold general election after far right pulls out of coalition

The Netherlands holds a general election after the far-right party of Geert Wilders dropped out of the ruling government coalition.

Tanzania holds presidential and parliamentary election

Tanzania holds its presidential and parliamentary election. Incumbent President Samia Suluhu Hassan will be expected to defend her post, which took upon the death of her predecessor John Magufuli in 2021.

Trial begins over Trump's decision to send troops to Portland, Oregon

A federal judge in Portland, Oregon will begin a three-day trial over Republican President Donald Trump's decision to send National Guard troops to the city, after previously ruling that the action was likely not justified. The judge will consider evidence about local protests against Trump's immigration enforcement policies, and the Trump administration's conclusion that the protests were preventing the enforcement of federal laws.

Trump expected to visit South Korea ahead of APEC summit

U.S. President Donald Trump will visit South Korea on October 29 and is expected to stay until October 30, South Korea's national security adviser said. He is expected to meet with China’s Xi Jinping.

03:30 - Soccer - MLS - Los Angeles FC v Austin FC

Game one of the MLS Playoffs Round One tie between Los Angeles FC and Austin FC at BMO Stadium.

04:00 - Malaysian, Australian leaders hold bilateral talks on the sidelines of the ASEAN Summit

Malaysian Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim and his Australian counterpart Anthony Albanese hold bilateral talks on the sidelines of the 47th ASEAN Leaders' Summit in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia.

08:00 - Chinese Foreign Ministry daily news conference

12:00 - PM takes questions in parliament

British Prime Minister Keir Starmer takes questions in parliament.

18:00 - HEALTH ROUNDS: A twice-a-week round-up of medical advances.

Brief summaries of medical breakthroughs, healthcare trends, pharmaceutical news and more, published on Wednesdays and Fridays.

19:00 - United States-Fed policy decision

THURSDAY OCTOBER 30, 2025

PREVIEW: A look at what is at stake in the New York mayoral election

Analysts weigh in on what is at stake in the November 4 New York mayoral election.

Trump meets China's Xi in South Korea

U.S. President Donald Trump is expected to meet Chinese President Xi Jinping in South Korea for a high-stakes summit.

Leaders arrive South Korea ahead of APEC meeting

Leaders of APEC countries arrive South Korea ahead of the annual meeting

The Paris Peace Forum

French President Emmanuel Macron is expected to participate in the Paris Peace Forum.

Japan's new PM is expected to visit South Korea for APEC

Japan's new prime minister, Sanae Takaichi, will visit South Korea from Nov. 30 for three days to attend the APEC Summit in Gyeongju.

Trump returns from Asia, attends Halloween at the White House

U.S. President Donald Trump returns from his Asia trip and attends Halloween at the White House, where he will welcome law enforcement, military and foster families, his spokesperson said.

Trump expected to visit South Korea ahead of APEC summit

U.S. President Donald Trump will visit South Korea on October 29 and is expected to stay until October 30, South Korea's national security adviser said. He is expected to meet with China’s Xi Jinping.

03:30 - Soccer - MLS - Los Angeles Football Club v Austin FC

2025 MLS Season (Playoff) - BMO Stadium, Los Angeles, California

08:00 - Newspapers and vox pops after Dutch election

Newspapers and vox pops in Amsterdam and The Hague after the Dutch go to the polls, in an election triggered by the far right party of Geert Wilders.

12:00 - Serbian government braces for protests on railway station collapse anniversary

Ahead of the first anniversary of the Novi Sad railway station roof collapse that killed 16 people, months of protests across Serbia have intensified calls for political change, rattling the leadership of President Aleksandar Vucic. The deadly accident in November last year, blamed by protesters on corruption and negligence, sparked nationwide demonstrations, including university shutdowns, urging early elections, aiming to end Vucic's 13-year rule and his Serbian Progressive Party's dominance.

12:00 - Interview with analyst after Dutch election

Interview with an analyst in The Hague after the Dutch go to the polls, in an election triggered by the far right party of Geert Wilders.

13:30 - United States-GDP Advance

22:00 - Ex-FBI chief Comey to bring more legal challenges to false statements case

Former FBI Director James Comey faces a deadline to file additional legal challenges to the criminal case accusing him of making false statements to Congress.

FRIDAY OCTOBER 31, 2025

Benin to announce presidential election candidates

Benin will release a list of candidates for a presidential election scheduled for April 12.

South Korea holds APEC leaders' summit

South Korea hosts the 2025 Asia-Pacific Economic Cooperation leaders' summit on October 31 and November 1.

09:30 - Hong Kong-GDP Advance

18:00 - HEALTH ROUNDS: A twice-a-week round-up of medical advances.

Brief summaries of medical breakthroughs, healthcare trends, pharmaceutical news and more, published on Wednesdays and Fridays.

18:00 - Status conference in Trump's defamation lawsuit against Wall Street Journal over Epstein story

A U.S. judge is due to hold a status conference on October 31 in President Donald Trump's defamation lawsuit against the Wall Street Journal over its article about a birthday greeting for the late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein bearing the president's signature. Coverage on merit.

SATURDAY NOVEMBER 1, 2025

South Korea holds APEC leaders' summit

South Korea hosts the 2025 Asia-Pacific Economic Cooperation leaders' summit on October 31 and November 1.

SUNDAY NOVEMBER 2, 2025

NO EVENTS SCHEDULED
